data.content:
Google L3 (Entry-Mid Level) Interview Pattern:

Format: 1 Phone Screen + 4-5 Onsite Rounds (3 Coding + 1 Googleyness & Leadership + 1 System Design light)

Key Focus Areas:
- Algorithmic thinking (Medium-Hard Leetcode)
- Code quality and edge case handling
- Googleyness: collaboration, humility, ability to navigate ambiguity

Most Common Algorithm Topics:
1. Arrays & Strings (sliding window, two pointers)
2. Trees & Graphs (BFS, DFS, shortest path)
3. Dynamic Programming (medium difficulty)
4. Recursion and Backtracking
5. Hash Maps for optimization

Googleyness Questions:
- Tell me about a time you had to work with someone difficult
- Describe a situation where you had to learn something quickly
- How do you handle ambiguous requirements?
- Tell me about a time you failed and what you learned

Tips:
- Think aloud during coding rounds — process matters as much as solution
- Always discuss time and space complexity
- Write clean, readable code — Google cares about code quality
- Ask clarifying questions before jumping into solutions
- Multiple correct approaches > one optimal approach rushed

Google L4 (Mid-Senior) Interview Pattern:

Format: 4-5 Onsite Rounds (2-3 Coding + 1 System Design + 1 Googleyness & Leadership)

System Design is critical at L4:
- Design Google Maps search
- Design Gmail
- Design YouTube video processing pipeline
- Design a web crawler

Expectations beyond L3:
- Demonstrate technical leadership
- Show impact beyond your immediate team
- Deep expertise in at least one area
- Design for scale (millions of users)

Tips:
- System Design: focus on scalability, reliability, and trade-offs
- Leadership: show examples of influencing without authority
- Coding: expected to solve hard problems cleanly
